name: antelop

metadata.name: antelop
metadata.summary: Antelope is a data management and processing tool for systems neuroscientists

documentation:
# Antelope
Antelope is a database for electrophysiology data. It provides a web based GUI, automatic spikesorting, and data analysis tools built in.

Please read our [documentation](https://antelope.readthedocs.io/en/latest/).

functions.name: antelope.scripts.app.chatgpt
documentation: @st.experimental_dialog('How can I help you?')

functions.name: antelope.scripts.find_port.is_port_available
documentation: Check if a port is available on the given host.

functions.name: antelope.scripts.find_port.find_available_port
documentation: Find an available port on the given host.

functions.name: antelope.scripts.plot_diagrams.remove_white_background
documentation: function to remove white background from the images and reshape

modules.name: antelope.analysis.firing_rate
documentation: This module contains the standard library firing rate analysis.

classes.name: antelope.analysis.firing_rate.SpikeCountRate
documentation:
Calculate the spike count rate of a neuron.
This is the number of spikes divided by the duration of the recording.

classes.name: antelope.analysis.firing_rate.FiringRate
documentation:
Smooth the spike train with a kernel.

Arguments:
kernel (np.ndarray[2,n]): The kernel to convolve the spike train with. Should consist of values and timestamps.
sample_rate (float): The sample rate of the spike train.

classes.name: antelope.analysis.firing_rate.GaussianSmoothing
documentation:
Smooth the spike train with a Gaussian kernel.

Arguments:
sigma (float): The standard deviation of the Gaussian kernel in seconds.
sample_rate (float): The sample rate of the output firing rate
truncate (float): The number of standard deviations to include in the kernel.

classes.name: antelope.analysis.firing_rate.RectangularSmoothing
documentation:
Smooth the spike train with a rectangular kernel.

Arguments:
width (float): The width of the kernel in seconds.
sample_rate (float): The sample rate of the output firing rate.

classes.name: antelope.analysis.firing_rate.ExponentialSmoothing
documentation:
Smooth the spike train with a exponential kernel.

Arguments:
tau (float): The time parameter of the kernel in seconds.
sample_rate (float): The sample rate of the output firing rate.
truncate (float): The number of time constants to include in the kernel.

classes.name: antelope.analysis.firing_rate.PlotSmoothed
documentation:
Smooth the spike train with a Gaussian kernel and plot.

Arguments:
sigma (float): The standard deviation of the Gaussian kernel in seconds.
sample_rate (float): The sample rate of the output firing rate
window_start (float): The start of the window to plot in seconds.
window_end (float): The end of the window to plot in seconds (default is -1 the end of the recording).
width (float): The width parameter of the kernel.
kernel (str): The type of kernel to use ('gaussian', 'rectangular', 'exponential').

classes.name: antelope.analysis.hello_world.Greeting
documentation: This is Antelope's hello world function.

classes.name: antelope.analysis.hello_world.CountExperiments
documentation:
This is a slightly more complex example showing how we can aggregate over another table and rename variables within the function.
It's worth noting that when you aggregate, the argument passed to the function will always be a list.

classes.name: antelope.analysis.hello_world.GreetingWithCount
documentation:
This example shows how we can build on top of other functions and use multiple attributes, both within the same table and from different tables.
To do so, we need to define the other functions we want to run in the `inherits` attribute, and pass them as inputs to the function.
These inner functions can then be run with any restriction - although the typical use case is to use a primary key.

classes.name: antelope.analysis.hello_world.FirstExperimentName
documentation:
This example shows how we can use a restriction to filter the data within the function.

Restrictions can of course be passed when running the function, but are useful at this level
to define when the function doesn't apply to certain attributes, or more commonly, to define
different subsets of aggregated attributes as different inputs to the function.

Note, you should always handle the case where the function input is an empty list.

classes.name: antelope.analysis.hello_world.ExampleFigure
documentation: Example of a function that returns a matplotlib figure.

classes.name: antelope.analysis.hello_world.Sta
documentation:
The spike-triggered average for an analog event.

This example shows how for some functions, it makes sense to define the function as running on the join of two tables.

modules.name: antelope.analysis.isi
documentation: This module contains the standard library isi ratio analysis functions.

classes.name: antelope.analysis.isi.IsiPlot
documentation:
Plot the interspike interval histogram of a spike train.

Arguments:
bin_size (float): The size of the bins in the histogram in seconds.
window (float): The length of the window to plot in seconds.

classes.name: antelope.analysis.isi.AutoCorrelogram
documentation:
Plot the interspike interval histogram of a spike train.

Arguments:
sample_rate (float): The sample rate of the autocorrelogram in Hz.

functions.name: antelope.connection.connect.dbconnect
documentation:
Function loads configuration from home and
returns a connection to the database.

functions.name: antelope.connection.connect.connect
documentation:
Function used by streamlit to connect to database and effectively
cache the connection.
Streamlit internal caching mechanism is not used because we had
a number of crashes, particularly when mutlithreading.

functions.name: antelope.connection.connect.check_credentials
documentation: check credentials and return connection

modules.name: antelope.analysis.sta
documentation: This module contains the standard library spike-triggered average analysis functions.

classes.name: antelope.analysis.sta.DigitalSta
documentation: The spike-triggered average for a digital event.

classes.name: antelope.analysis.sta.PlotDigitalSta
documentation: Plot the spike-triggered average for an analog event.

classes.name: antelope.analysis.sta.IntervalSta
documentation: The spike-triggered average for a digital event.

classes.name: antelope.analysis.sta.PlotIntervalSta
documentation: Plot the spike-triggered average for an interval event.

modules.name: antelope.connection.permission
documentation:
Script contains functions to enforce user permissions.
These will overwrite the table methods via monkey patching.

functions.name: antelope.connection.permission.safe_delete_python
documentation:
Safely deletes data from the database (just changes the 'delete' attribute to True)
These deletes cascade
Inputs: query: the query to delete

classes.name: antelope.connection.permission.PermissionDeniedError
documentation: Exception raised when a user lacks permission to perform an action.

functions.name: antelope.utils.analysis_utils.patch_functions
documentation:
This function patches the functions to allow other functions and tables to be accessible in the run() method.
Has to be separate from the decorator as functions haven't yet been initialised with connections when the decorator is called.

functions.name: antelope.utils.analysis_utils.split_trials
documentation:
Split trials based on mask

Inputs:
mask: tuple of data, timestamps
data: tuple of data, timestamps

Returns:
list of tuples of data, timestamps

functions.name: antelope.utils.analysis_utils.instantiate_function
documentation: Function loads an analysis function from the masks table and returns an instantiation of it

functions.name: antelope.utils.analysis_utils.get_docstring
documentation: Gets docstring from function string

functions.name: antelope.utils.analysis_utils.reload_analysis
documentation: Reloads analysis functions and repatches them if the underlying script has changed

functions.name: antelope.utils.hash_utils.hash_query
documentation:
Function produces a reproducible hash of a query.
It uses the MD5 algorithm on the database side and is therefore quite fast.

functions.name: antelope.utils.hash_utils.hash_tables
documentation:
Function hashes the data across several tables belonging to a given key.
This can be used to check if data is consistent fo a given key across the database.

Arguments:
tables: list of tables to hash
key: dict giving a datajoint restriction

functions.name: antelope.utils.hash_utils.code_hash
documentation: Function hashes the code of a function.

functions.name: antelope.utils.hash_utils.list_to_datajoint
documentation: Function to convert a list of tablenames to a list of datajoint tables.

functions.name: antelope.utils.hash_utils.dfs_functions
documentation:
Does a depth first search to find the graph of all functions that are called recursively.
Assumes functions are called in a tree. Could be modified if loops are allowed later.

functions.name: antelope.utils.hash_utils.dfs_functions_not_tables
documentation: Just returns all functions that are called recursively.

functions.name: antelope.utils.visualisation_utils.plot_isi
documentation: Plot the isi of the selected units

functions.name: antelope.utils.visualisation_utils.unit_stats
documentation: Computes some statistics for each unit

functions.name: antelope.utils.antelope_utils.validate_behaviour_rig
documentation:
Function validates an input behavior rig specification against our custom schema
\:param data: dict
\:return: Bool

functions.name: antelope.utils.antelope_utils.validate_mask
documentation:
Function validates an input behavior rig specification against our custom schema
\:param data: dict
\:return: Bool

functions.name: antelope.utils.antelope_utils.insert_features
documentation:
Function populates the features table from the json file,
leaving the feature empty

functions.name: antelope.utils.antelope_utils.upload_rig_json
documentation:
Function validates the rig json, converts and uploads it along with corresponding features

Inputs:
    experimenter (str): experimenter username
    rig_name (str): name of the rig
    rig_json (path): path to the rig json file
    masklist (list of dict): list of mask dictionaries, with keys 'name', 'description', 'function'

functions.name: antelope.utils.antelope_utils.make_timestamps
documentation: Function makes timestamps for analog events

functions.name: antelope.utils.antelope_utils.check_nwb
documentation: Function reads an NWB file and the database features and checks the nwb file is valid

functions.name: antelope.utils.antelope_utils.check_streamlit
documentation:
Function to check whether python code is run within streamlit

Returns
-------
use_streamlit : boolean
    True if code is run within streamlit, else False

functions.name: antelope.utils.antelope_utils.insert_nwb
documentation:
Function reads an NWB file and the database features and inserts the data into the database

Args:
    session (dict): session primary key
    animals (list of dict): animal primary keys
    nwbpath (Path): path to the nwb file

Returns:
    None

functions.name: antelope.utils.antelope_utils.check_animals
documentation: Function reads our custom rig schema and checks how many animals are in the rig

functions.name: antelope.utils.antelope_utils.check_dlc_folder
documentation: Function checks if a dlc folder exists and is valid.

functions.name: antelope.utils.antelope_utils.create_dlc_folder
documentation:
This function creates a dlc folder for a specific experiment.
If it already exists, it checks the videos, and if they all match, it returns the config path.
Otherwise, it deletes the DLC folder, downloads the remaining videos, makes the DLC folder and returns the config path.

functions.name: antelope.utils.antelope_utils.upload_dlc
documentation: Function zips the labelled frames folder and uploads to the database along with the config json

functions.name: antelope.utils.antelope_utils.select_masks
documentation: Function displays a streamlit data editor that allows the user to select the masks they want to compute

functions.name: antelope.utils.antelope_utils.insert_masks
documentation: Function makes dataframe out of masks

functions.name: antelope.utils.antelope_utils.display_dlc_images
documentation: Function displays the train and test images for a DLCModel

functions.name: antelope.utils.antelope_utils.delete_masks
documentation: Computes masks to be deleted and permanently deletes them

functions.name: antelope.utils.antelope_utils.export_nwb
documentation: Function exports the NWB file for a session

classes.name: antelope.utils.antelope_utils.SafeNWBContext
documentation:
Context manager deletes NWB file if an error occurs
Designed so that the file should be opened within this context, several times if necessary for memory reasons.

modules.name: antelope.utils.streamlit_utils
documentation:
Additional utilities for antelope's web gui, written in streamlit.

Author: Rory Bedford
Date: 07/07/23

functions.name: antelope.utils.streamlit_utils.display_sorting_parameters
documentation:
Function which displays the spikesorting parameters selected
Inputs: paramdict: dictionary with sorting params

functions.name: antelope.utils.streamlit_utils.waveform_params_input
documentation: Function retrieves waveform extraction parameters

functions.name: antelope.utils.streamlit_utils.lfp_params_input
documentation: Function retrieves LFP extraction parameters

functions.name: antelope.utils.streamlit_utils.agreement_params_input
documentation:
Function to allow interactive input of spikesorting agreement matching parameters.
Inputs: numsorters: number of spikesorters
Returns: agreement_params: dict with parameters

functions.name: antelope.utils.streamlit_utils.preprocessing_params_input
documentation:
Function to allow interactive input of spikesorting preprocessing parameters.
Inputs: None
Returns: preprocessing_parameters: dict with parameters

functions.name: antelope.utils.streamlit_utils.interactive_sorter_params_input
documentation:
Function to allow interactive input of spikesorting parameters
for a number of different spikesorters.
Inputs: default: default values for each parameter (dict)
        desc: descriptions for each parameter (dict)
        parent_key: used for recursion as some dicts are nested
Returns: parameters: dict with parameters for this sorter

functions.name: antelope.utils.streamlit_utils.input_sorter_params
documentation:
Higher level function that gets sorter parameters for all spikesorters
Inputs: None
Returns: sorter_params: json with parameters

functions.name: antelope.utils.streamlit_utils.dropdown_query_table
documentation:
A custom streamlit widget that allows users to interactively query an existing
table's primary keys for selection of an entry

Displays: interactive dropdown selectboxes
Returns: the primary key selected

functions.name: antelope.utils.streamlit_utils.dropdown_insert_table
documentation:
A custom streamlit widget that allows users to interactively insert a new manual entry into the database. It involves querying the table's parents to see possible values.

Inputs: tables: structure of the database
        existing_dict: entries can be pre-entered (such as experimenter)
Displays: interactive dropdown selectboxes
Returns: the dictionary of values to insert

functions.name: antelope.utils.streamlit_utils.server_directory_browser
documentation:
A custom Streamlit widget that allows the user to browse directories on the server side.

Returns: The selected directory path. Returns None if no directory is selected.

functions.name: antelope.utils.streamlit_utils.add_video
documentation:
Function to allow interactive input of video parameters
Inputs: None
Returns: video_params: dict with parameters

functions.name: antelope.utils.streamlit_utils.add_feature
documentation:
Function to allow interactive input of feature parameters
Inputs: None
Returns: feature_params: dict with parameters

functions.name: antelope.utils.streamlit_utils.default_feature
documentation: Function to return default feature parameters

functions.name: antelope.utils.streamlit_utils.default_video
documentation: Function to return default video parameters

functions.name: antelope.utils.streamlit_utils.change_num_features
documentation: If the user changes the number of features, we need to update the feature_params dict in the session state

functions.name: antelope.utils.streamlit_utils.change_num_videos
documentation: If the user changes the number of videos, we need to update the video_params dict in the session state

functions.name: antelope.utils.streamlit_utils.define_rig_json
documentation:
Function to allow interactive input of behaviour rig parameters
Inputs: None
Returns: rig_dict: dict with parameters

functions.name: antelope.utils.streamlit_utils.enter_args
documentation:
Function to allow interactive input of analysis function arguments
Inputs: args: dict with arguments
Returns: args: dict with user input

functions.name: antelope.utils.streamlit_utils.display_analysis
documentation: Function to display the results of an analysis function

functions.name: antelope.utils.streamlit_utils.children_buttons
documentation: Function displays buttons to navigate to children table

functions.name: antelope.utils.analysis_base.antelope_analysis
documentation: This is a class decorator used to reduce boilerplate code in analysis functions.

methods.name: antelope.utils.analysis_base.AnalysisBase.save_result
documentation: Runs the function, and saves the pickled result to disk along with the reproducibility json.

methods.name: antelope.utils.analysis_base.AnalysisBase.reproduce
documentation:
Loads a reproducibility json and runs the function with the same arguments,
checking the data hash and code hash.

methods.name: antelope.utils.analysis_base.AnalysisBase.check_hash
documentation:
Loads a reproducibility json and runs the function with the same arguments,
checking the data hash and code hash.

modules.name: antelope.utils.datajoint_utils
documentation:
Utility functions for datajoint table manipulations

Author: Rory Bedfod
Date: 07/07/23

functions.name: antelope.utils.datajoint_utils.delete_status
documentation:
Function returns the delete status of a table
Options include 'temp', 'perm', 'none', 'custom-behave'

functions.name: antelope.utils.datajoint_utils.check_spare_key
documentation: Function checks if there's a spare key in the table primary keys, not belonging to any parent

functions.name: antelope.utils.datajoint_utils.insertable_tables
documentation: Returns the subdictionary of all tables the user can insert into

functions.name: antelope.utils.datajoint_utils.searchable_tables
documentation: Returns the subdictionary of tables the user is able to search

functions.name: antelope.utils.datajoint_utils.form_query
documentation:
Forms a valid datajoint query object from an input string
Supports all the tables, operators, and restrictions
Should return errors on any misformed queries (provides some protection against malignant SQL)
Inputs: querystring: string representing datajoint query
Returns: query: a valid datajoint query object

functions.name: antelope.utils.datajoint_utils.parent_primaries
documentation:
Finds the parent tables referenced by all foreign keys for a given table

Inputs: table: the table to apply the operation to
        tables: a dictionary of all tables in the database
        primary_only: if True, only return for primary keys of the table

Returns: a dictionary of attribute:table mappings for all foreign keys

functions.name: antelope.utils.datajoint_utils.ancestor_primaries
documentation:
Finds the earliest ancestor tables referenced by all foreign keys for a given table

Inputs: table: the table to apply the operation to
        tables: a dictionary of all tables in the database

Returns: a dictionary of attribute:table mappings for all foreign keys

functions.name: antelope.utils.datajoint_utils.query_without_external
documentation:
Queries the database without downloading external attributes or displaying jsons

Inputs: table: table to be queried
        restriction: optional dict of restrictions

Outputs: dataframe of query with up to 30 items (with placeholders for external and json)
         length of full query

functions.name: antelope.utils.datajoint_utils.download_data
documentation:
Downloads query as a numpy array as well as external data.
Inputs: querystring: the query in string form (needs to be serialisble - queries themselves include connections)
        download_path: path to download data to

functions.name: antelope.utils.datajoint_utils.check_session
documentation: function checks if directory has correct extension

functions.name: antelope.utils.datajoint_utils.upload
documentation:
Uploads data to database including external data
Inputs: table: the table to insert to
        insert_dict: the content of the upload as a dictionary

functions.name: antelope.utils.datajoint_utils.safe_delete
documentation:
Safely deletes data from the database (just changes the 'delete' attribute to True)
These deletes cascade
Inputs: tablename: the table to delete from
        restriction: the key to delete

functions.name: antelope.utils.datajoint_utils.restore
documentation:
Restores deleted data from the database (just changes the 'delete' attribute to False)
These restores cascade
Inputs: querystring: the query to restore from

functions.name: antelope.utils.datajoint_utils.release_computation
documentation:
Releases data from computation(just changes the 'in_compute' attribute to False)
Inputs: querystring: the query to release from

functions.name: antelope.utils.datajoint_utils.true_delete
documentation:
Deletes data from the database
Inputs: query: the query to delete

functions.name: antelope.utils.datajoint_utils.show_deletes
documentation: Function to show all the deletes that cascade

functions.name: antelope.utils.datajoint_utils.show_restores
documentation: Function to show all the restores that cascade

functions.name: antelope.utils.datajoint_utils.show_true_deletes
documentation: Function to show all the restores that cascade

functions.name: antelope.utils.datajoint_utils.query_to_str
documentation:
Function makes an easily readable string from a datajoint query
Used for file names and identifying queries

classes.name: antelope.utils.datajoint_utils.SimulateEnterContext
documentation: some datajoint functions seem to be broken and require user input

functions.name: antelope.utils.external_utils.open_phy

functions.name: antelope.utils.external_utils.schedule_train_dlc

functions.name: antelope.utils.external_utils.train_dlc_progress

functions.name: antelope.utils.external_utils.schedule_analysis
documentation: Function sends analysis job to cluster.

functions.name: antelope.utils.external_utils.analysis_progress
